response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack and DLang developer with broad web/cloud experience, but the role demands native-level Japanese, deep C/UNIX embedded firmware work, Linux kernel modification, and switch/networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch chip SDKs). The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments a decade ago and no Japanese language ability, embedded firmware, or network-switch security background, making this a poor fit for a mid-senior embedded security role.
strengths: Strong general software engineering background with 10+ years across multiple stacks
Some historical C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Linux familiarity and DevOps/CI experience relevant to embedded development environments
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement is native-level)
No embedded firmware, switch-chip SDK, MACsec/802.1X, or L2/L3 switch security experience
No Linux kernel source modification or kernel vulnerability work shown; C is not a current primary language

key_factors.factor: Lacks embedded/switch security and kernel experience
Strong general engineering and Linux background
